incidents.victim: Adobe Acrobat and Reader users worldwide
incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day CVE-2026-34621 (CVSS 8.6) in Adobe Acrobat and Reader via malicious PDF files requiring no user interaction beyond opening the document.
incidents.impact: Actively exploited since at least November 2025 — over four months before Adobe patched it. Exploit silently fingerprints victims before optionally delivering remote code execution and sandbox escape payloads.

incidents.victim: Government agencies, military, critical infrastructure worldwide (200+ organizations)
incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in 18,000+ end-of-life SOHO routers (TP-Link, Mikrotik) to hijack DNS settings and intercept OAuth authentication tokens via adversary-in-the-middle attacks on Microsoft 365.
incidents.impact: Harvested credentials, emails, and authentication tokens from 200+ organizations and 5,000 consumer devices across 120+ countries without deploying malware. FBI conducted court-authorized 'Operation Masquerade' to remediate compromised U.S. routers.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS 9.1) in Fortinet FortiClient EMS allowing improper access control and unauthenticated remote code execution.
incidents.impact: CISA added to KEV catalog and ordered federal agencies to patch immediately. Second FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks. Exploitation ramped up over Easter weekend when security teams were at reduced staffing.

incidents.victim: University of Mississippi Medical Center; Passaic County, New Jersey
incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware operators exploited web-facing vulnerabilities including zero-days in SmarterMail and GoAnywhere MFT, achieving data exfiltration and encryption within 24 hours of initial access.
incidents.impact: UMMC, the largest hospital in Mississippi, was forced to divert patients and shut down systems; it fully reopened March 2 with FBI and DHS assistance. Passaic County also claimed as victim. Microsoft warns Medusa increasingly targets healthcare and municipal government.

incidents.victim: Jones Day (major U.S. law firm, 2,400 lawyers)
incidents.vector: Phishing attack by Silent Ransom Group targeting firm personnel, gaining access to a limited number of dated client files.
incidents.impact: Client materials for 10 clients accessed and posted online on March 30. The firm represented President Trump in his 2016 and 2020 campaigns. FBI has warned Silent Ransom Group has been targeting law firms since 2023 due to the sensitive nature of legal data.

incidents.victim: Minot, North Dakota water treatment plant
incidents.vector: Ransomware deployment targeting IT systems at the city's water treatment facility.
incidents.impact: IT systems encrypted but the water plant remained operationally functional. Represents continued ransomware targeting of U.S. water infrastructure.

incidents.victim: Southeast Asian government entities (dozens of agencies)
incidents.vector: Exploited vulnerability CVE-2026-3502 in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute malicious payloads through trusted update channels to connected government endpoints.
incidents.impact: Compromised TrueConf on-premises server operated by governmental IT department distributed malicious updates to dozens of government entities. Attackers deployed ShadowPad malware and Havoc penetration testing tool for persistent access.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP hackers breached the European Commission's AWS account by exploiting a compromised version of the Trivy security scanner received through normal software update channels, then misused a stolen Amazon API key.
incidents.impact: Approximately 92 GB of compressed data stolen including 52,000 email communication files. Data from 42 internal clients and 29 EU entities potentially exposed. Stolen data appeared on ShinyHunters dark web site on March 28.

incidents.victim: Hundreds of organizations running Next.js applications
incidents.vector: Large-scale automated campaign exploiting React2Shell RCE vulnerability (CVE-2025-55182) in Next.js applications using a custom framework called 'NEXUS Listener' to extract credentials from compromised hosts.
incidents.impact: Attackers automatically harvested cloud tokens, database credentials, and SSH keys from hundreds of compromised hosts to facilitate further malicious activity. Creates significant risks for lateral movement and supply chain integrity.

incidents.vector: Wiper attack launched by Iranian government-connected group Handala in retaliation over the US-Israel conflict, targeting the company's core business systems.
incidents.impact: Order processing, manufacturing, and shipping disrupted for three weeks. Company reported being 'fully operational' by early April. FBI seized Handala-associated websites and State Department offered a reward for information on the group.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack by XP95 group, a new ransomware operation that began operations in March 2026.
incidents.impact: Government statistics agency extorted for $100,000. XP95 is a newly emerged ransomware group representing the continued expansion of the ransomware ecosystem.

incidents.victim: Ashby, Dunstable, Pepperell, and Townsend, Massachusetts (police and fire departments)
incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the shared Patriot Regional Emergency Communications Center that serves the four towns' police and fire departments.
incidents.impact: Police and fire department operations disrupted across four Massachusetts towns. Attack traced to the compromise of a shared emergency communications center.

incidents.vector: Hackers obtained multisig transaction approvals from the Drift Security Council over weeks of preparation, then drained crypto-assets from the platform.
incidents.impact: $280 million in crypto-assets stolen in a carefully planned operation. The weeks-long preparation suggests sophisticated social engineering or insider compromise of the multisig approval process.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized network access identified on March 28; specific intrusion method not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Hasbro took systems offline and activated business continuity plan. Order fulfillment delays expected for several weeks. Company still assessing full scope of impact. Shares fell 3% in premarket trading.

incidents.victim: Critical infrastructure organizations in Israel, UAE, and broader Middle East (300+ organizations)
incidents.vector: Wide-ranging password spray campaign targeting Microsoft 365 accounts across satellite, aviation, energy, maritime, and municipal emergency services sectors. Targeting correlated with cities struck by Iranian missiles.
incidents.impact: Over 300 organizations targeted across three waves since early March 2026. Campaign assessed as likely supporting kinetic military operations and bombing damage assessment, representing deliberate integration of cyber operations with military strikes.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of use-after-free vulnerability CVE-2026-5281 in Chrome's Dawn WebGPU implementation — the fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks in 2026.
incidents.impact: CISA added to KEV catalog and ordered federal agencies to patch. Google issued emergency update. Vulnerability enables remote code execution via crafted web content.

incidents.victim: Axios JavaScript library users (estimated 600,000 downloads of compromised versions)
incidents.vector: Hijacked the npm account of the lead Axios maintainer via social engineering, then published malicious versions (v1.14.1, v0.30.4) containing a hidden dependency that deployed cross-platform remote access trojans.
incidents.impact: RAT malware deployed to Windows, macOS, and Linux systems with potential reach into millions of environments. Approximately 600,000 downloads in a three-hour window. Axios has 100 million weekly downloads. Google attributed attack to North Korean group UNC1069.

incidents.victim: Aqua Security Trivy, Checkmarx KICS, BerriAI LiteLLM, Telnyx SDK, European Commission, Mercor, 500,000+ machines
incidents.vector: TeamPCP systematically compromised open-source security tools (Trivy, KICS) and AI gateway (LiteLLM) by injecting infostealer payloads into GitHub Actions and PyPI packages, then harvested credentials to breach downstream targets.
incidents.impact: Over 300 GB of data exfiltrated from approximately 500,000 machines including cloud tokens, SSH keys, and Kubernetes secrets. Downstream victims include the European Commission's AWS environment and AI startup Mercor. Data sold to ShinyHunters and leaked on dark web.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of critical vulnerability CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) in Citrix NetScaler ADC enabling unauthenticated disclosure of sensitive memory, similar to previous Citrix Bleed attacks.
incidents.impact: CISA ordered immediate federal patching. Researchers compared impact to original Citrix Bleed (2023) which was used by ransomware gangs and nation-state hackers against hospitals and critical infrastructure. The NetScaler Gateway serves as the authentication front door for many organizations.
